meta.title: G30 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW 5 Series G30 engine — from the 2.0 B48 petrol to the 3.0 B58 and 4.4 N63 V8. Real UK costs, honest reliability data. Compare prices from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 5 Series G30 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned G30 engine costs between £2,800 and £10,000 supply-only, depending on the engine — the B47 diesel ranges £2,800–£5,200, the B48/B58 petrols run £3,200–£8,000, and the N63 V8 runs £6,000–£10,000. Fitting adds 10–22 hours of labour at £65–£90/hr. Use our calculator for a personalised quote.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 5 Series G30 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58B30 3.0-litre six-cylinder (540i) is widely regarded as BMW's most reliable modern six-cylinder — proven durability, no major design flaws, and only minor cooling system maintenance required. On diesel, the B57D30 (530d) is also highly reliable, with a robust timing chain design and excellent longevity.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 5 Series G30?

acceptedAnswer.text: EGR cooler failure on the B47 diesel (pre-recall units), cooling system leaks (water pump, expansion tank) on the B48 and B58 petrols, valve stem seal wear on the N63 V8 (M550i), and timing chain wear on high-mileage B57 diesels. The G30 also suffers from electric steering rack issues and suspension component wear on higher-mileage cars.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 5 Series G30 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the G30's modular engines (B38, B48, B58, B47, B57) are significantly more reliable than the N-series engines they replaced. The N63 V8 is the exception — it's a high-performance engine that demands meticulous maintenance. For any G30, a thorough engine inspection and maintenance history check are essential.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 5 Series G30 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: Early N63 V8 (2018–2020 M550i) without documented valve stem seal work carries the highest risk. Also avoid any B47 diesel without documented EGR cooler recall completion. If you must have a V8, choose a 2020+ N63B44D with full service history and budget for valve stem seals at 60,000 miles.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my G30 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Coolant loss or overheating (B48/B58 or B47 EGR cooler failure), blue smoke on startup (N63 valve stem seals), timing chain rattle on high-mileage B57 diesels, or persistent rough running on any high-mileage unit are the key warning signs. Use our diagnostic tool for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 5 Series G30 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply the 70% BMW scrap threshold. Against a well-kept £15,000 G30, a £4,500 B57 recon is 30% of the car's value — safe. Against a tired £7,000 early example, the same recon is 64% — borderline. For sub-£5,000 examples, we'd recommend walking away.

mainEntity.name: How long do BMW 5 Series G30 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58 and B48 engines can comfortably exceed 150,000 miles with routine maintenance. The B57 diesel is equally durable, with 200,000+ miles achievable with proper servicing. The N63 V8 requires more frequent maintenance — valve stem seals are a known wear item at 40,000–80,000 miles.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the G30 and other 5 Series generations?

acceptedAnswer.text: The G30 (2016–2023) is the seventh-generation 5 Series and the first to use BMW's CLAR platform. It replaced the F10 (2010–2017) and was succeeded by the G60 (2023–present). The G30 introduced significant weight savings, electric power steering, and a range of plug-in hybrid options. The G30 was also the first 5 Series to offer the 545e xDrive plug-in hybrid with a six-cylinder petrol.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 5 Series G30 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is listed on the VIN plate, vehicle documentation, and on a sticker under the bonnet. For 2.0 petrol, look for B48B16 (520i) or B48B20 (520i/530i). For 3.0 petrol, look for B58B30M0 (540i). For 4.4 V8, look for N63B44 (M550i). For 2.0 diesel, look for B47D20 (518d–525d). For 3.0 diesel, look for B57D30 (530d/540d) or B57D30C (M550d). For PHEV, look for B48B20 PHEV (530e) or B58B30M1 PHEV (545e). Tell us your variant and year, and we'll confirm.

hero.h1: BMW 5 Series G30 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the G30-generation BMW 5 Series — from the 2.0-litre B48 four-cylinder to the 3.0-litre B58 six and the 4.4-litre N63 V8. Real UK costs, honest failure data, and the B57 diesel's defining role in this generation's ownership story. Backed by 300+ real UK 5 Series enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: BMW 5 Series G30 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the G30 saloon specifically — from the 2016 launch through the 2020 LCI facelift and the introduction of mild-hybrid and plug-in hybrid variants.

overview.intro: The BMW G30 is the seventh generation of the BMW 5 Series, produced from November 2016 to June 2023. Built on BMW's CLAR (Cluster Architecture) platform, the G30 introduced significant weight savings through increased use of aluminium and high-strength steel, and offered a range of turbocharged petrol, diesel, and plug-in hybrid powertrains. The engine line-up evolved substantially over its production run: early cars used B48 four-cylinder and B58 six-cylinder petrols, alongside B47 four-cylinder and B57 six-cylinder diesels. The 2020 Life Cycle Impulse (LCI) facelift brought mild-hybrid technology to many models, increased power outputs, and introduced the 545e xDrive plug-in hybrid with the B58 six-cylinder petrol. The G30 was succeeded by the G60 5 Series in 2023.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: BMW CLAR (Cluster Architecture) • Predecessor: BMW 5 Series F10 (2010–2017) • Successor: BMW 5 Series G60 (2023–present) • Sibling body styles in this generation: G31 Touring
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The G30 5 Series generated 300+ real UK engine enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED], concentrated on the 2.0-litre B47 diesel and B48 petrol engines as the parc exits warranty and high-mileage maintenance comes to the fore.

items.engine: B57D30 3.0-litre six-cylinder (530d) — The 261–282 bhp B57 six-cylinder diesel delivers effortless torque, excellent refinement, and genuine durability. It's the engine that defines the G30 as a motorway cruiser — 620 Nm of torque and 0-60 in 5.8 seconds with 50+ mpg economy.
items.whoItsFor: high-mileage buyers who want six-cylinder refinement without petrol running costs.

items.engine: B58B30 3.0-litre six-cylinder (540i) — The 335 bhp B58 is widely regarded as BMW's most reliable modern six-cylinder. Delivering 450 Nm of torque and 0-60 in 5.1 seconds, it offers genuine performance with proven durability.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want the sweet spot of performance, refinement, and reliability.

items.engine: N63B44 4.4-litre V8 (M550i) — The hot-vee V8 design places turbochargers in the engine's 'V', generating significant heat. Valve stem seal wear and cooling system leaks are well-documented on early N63 units. The updated N63B44D (2020+) is improved but still demands meticulous maintenance.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who accept the premium and keep the car under warranty.

items.engine: N63B44 V8 (M550i) — The 4.4-litre V8 is the most expensive engine in the G30 line-up to replace, with reconditioned units costing £6,000–£10,000. Valve stem seal replacement is a labour-intensive job — £3,000–£6,000 at a specialist — and cooling system failures can lead to catastrophic engine damage.
items.whoItsFor: buyers with deep pockets and a commitment to specialist servicing.

items.engine: B47D20 2.0-litre diesel (520d) — The 188 bhp B47 diesel offers the best balance of performance, economy, and running costs. It's the most common G30 engine, parts are widely available, and the recon cost is the lowest in the range. Post-recall EGR cooler units are reliable, and DPF issues are manageable with regular motorway driving.
items.whoItsFor: the majority of G30 buyers who prioritise running costs.

items.engine: B48B20 2.0-litre petrol (530i) — Often overshadowed by the six-cylinder 540i, the 248 bhp B48 delivers genuine performance — 0-60 in 6.2 seconds — with the economy of a four-cylinder and, crucially, the proven reliability of BMW's modular engine family.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want petrol refinement without the six-cylinder's fuel consumption.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Highest Risk — V8 ownership is expensive

economicsRule.title: The G30 rule
economicsRule.text: A £4,500–£8,000 B57 recon against a £8,000–£12,000 early G30 sits at 38–100% of the car's value. On a sub-£8,000 example, that brushes our 70% BMW threshold — we'll honestly tell you to walk away. Against a well-kept £15,000+ example, the same recon drops to a routine 30–53% — a repair decision, not a financial one. Value the car first.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G30 Costs Least To Own?
Which G30 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: N63 M550i — valve stem seals and cooling system repairs are expensive.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G30 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: B47 520d — the sweet spot between economy, performance, and running costs.

problems.title: B47 Diesel EGR Cooler Failure
problems.rootCause: The EGR cooler on B47 diesel engines can develop internal coolant leaks, leading to coolant loss and, in rare cases, fire risk. BMW issued a recall for this issue — any car without documented recall work carries this risk.
problems.recommendation: On any G30 diesel, check the EGR cooler recall status immediately — a £500–£1,200 preventative repair is cheaper than the £2,800–£5,000 replacement that follows if ignored.

cta.label: Compare B47 engine prices for your G30 →

problems.title: B48/B58 Cooling System Leaks
problems.rootCause: The cooling system on B48 and B58 engines — including the expansion tank, water pump, and plastic fittings — can develop leaks over time. Coolant loss can lead to overheating and, if ignored, engine damage.

tieredCosts.work: Water pump, expansion tank, coolant pipes

problems.recommendation: On any G30 petrol approaching 60,000 miles, have the cooling system inspected — a £300–£800 preventative repair is cheaper than the £3,200–£6,000 replacement that follows if ignored.

cta.label: Compare B48/B58 engine prices for your G30 →

problems.title: N63 V8 Valve Stem Seal Wear
problems.rootCause: The hot-vee V8 design generates significant heat, causing valve stem seals to harden and wear prematurely. Blue smoke on startup is the classic warning sign; ignored, it leads to oil consumption and potential catalytic converter damage.
problems.recommendation: On any M550i, watch for blue smoke on startup — a £3,000–£5,000 preventative repair is cheaper than the £6,000–£10,000 replacement that follows if ignored.

cta.label: Compare N63 engine prices for your G30 →

replacementCosts.h2: G30 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are G30 saloon only. G31 Touring shares most of these engines but is covered on its own Generation Page. The LCI facelift (2020) introduced mild-hybrid technology to many models, increased power outputs, and added the 545e xDrive plug-in hybrid with the B58 six-cylinder. The M550i xDrive received the updated N63B44D V8 in 2020, increasing power from 456 bhp to 523 bhp.

mostCommonFailures: 3. N63 V8 valve stem seal wear
